Categories: Restaurants, Soup, Japanese, Sushi Bars | Review: I tried, I really tried. I have been here several times, hoping that it would improve but it hasn't. Terrible service, they were out of oysters and crab, the Nigiri had way too much rice, the edamame was overcooked. What a waste. We live close by but we'll make the drive to Sushi Teri next time

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Dinner here was such a great experience! The service was amazing. You could tell our server wanted to really make our experience perfect. 
The food was delicious as well! We enjoyed everything we ate. We ordered the French onion soup, pork belly, wagyu beef, and scallop trio. All of them were really tasty.
I loved that the chef delivered our entree and asked us if we had any questions. 
All around good experience.

Categories: American (New), Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Why on earth would you ever want to eat here for breakfast? My girlfriend and I went there for breakfast and the woman who took our order couldn't of been any more friendlier - yes, I'm  being very sarcastic. We ordered our food at the window and she was acting as if she had more important things to do than serve us. Apparently customers aren't important here. I ordered the chicken, gravy and biscuits - and potatoes and bacon - which our side dishes came out first. The best thing on the menu was the bacon . Even getting silverware was a hassle. The only person who was decent to us was a smiling woman who was cleaning up afterbus and she promptly provided us a box. Honestly, I ate there an hour ago and I feel gross and sluggish. Is this SB restaurant trying too hard to be like LA? Nice try but even similar restaurants in LA have better service and items on their menu. Definitely not worth it!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: A recommendation. A very good one! I hear the Gorditas are great, but I went with the adobada (adovada?) sope. Well seasoned fresh, and quality meat. A little pricey for one item with no sides or drink, portion was very nice though. They have a small salsa bar as most places should have. Small interior, seating about 10, very clean. Quick service, if I lived closer I'd come here a bit more to try the other goodies. I hear the tacos are great as well. 

Where I was raised sope is one of my fav dishes. My new home in Los Angeles county turned me on to adovada (adobada?). I don't know which is the correct spelling, I'll look it up. Since I love those two things I decided to get this combo. This version of this dish and meat prep are drastically different than what I am accustomed to, but that's not a bad thing. You won't regret eating here.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Chicken Shop, Chicken Wings, Fast Food, Caterers, Event Planning & Services | Review: I love the spicy chicken sandwich.  The crosscut fries are good too but are mostly used to get more delicious Chick-fil-A sauce in my mouth.

The place is always clean with great service.

I haven't been to impressed with the dessert options though.  The cookie sundae was recommended to us but the ice cream was more ice than cream and the cookie chunks were a little hard to chew on.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: This is definitely my GO-TO lunch spot.  Friendly service, delicious food, and reasonable pricing.  I was first introduced to this place by a friend who goes every Tuesday and Friday for the Veggie Korma in their buffet.  As I transition to being more vegetarian, It's great to a variety of delicious veggie options as well as health meat ones if I'm feeling tempted :). Also comes with a piping hot Naan bread which is to die for.

I met the owner Remi the other day and his smile/nod of acknowledgement every time I come in makes me feel like I'm family.

Highly recommend this to anyone!

Categories: American (New), Tapas/Small Plates, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants, Burgers | Review: I thought that this was just oKay. I see why people like it with all the craft beers and the outside atmosphere, but it's a lot of money for ordering at a counter and having your food come out all at different times. The place didn't flow and the food was okay for the exception of the Mexican Street corn which  was awesome and a must try but it didn't come out till my husband and and son were already done with their food and it's supposed to be a starter.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Karaoke, Beer Bar, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Just went here and I am extremely disappointed. First of all, I asked about the Carne Asada fries and I was told that they come with "fries, CHEESE and steak". That's fine, sounds normal. I decided I would rather get the Carne Asada tacos and I asked what comes on them, and I was told that it only comes with the meat. I asked if I could get lettuce and cheese and the cashier responded "lettuce is at the salsa bar and we do not have CHEESE". You do not have CHEESE? FOR TACOS? When I was told literally 10 seconds prior that they serve cheese on the fries??? I do not understand. So I proceeded with my order anyways, and I got my tacos and headed to the salsa bar. The lettuce is not lettuce, it is actually parsley MIXED with onions (???). They do not have lettuce by itself. Lastly, my receipt says I was charged $0.15 for "1 Sorry". I have no idea what a "Sorry" is, nor did I purchase one. I asked what this meant and apparently it is the sales tax, but on the receipt the sales tax was listed separately at $0.47. This place is a complete joke and might as well close down until they can get the ingredients necessary for tacos, as this is a taco place right? Terrible.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Bars, Nightlife, Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Desserts, Food | Review: GREAT FOOD, FRIENDLY SERVICE
I can't begin to list all that makes this restaurant worthwhile, but I will try:

The staff are friendly and love being there! They are "like family" as was explained
They REALLY care that you are happy and enjoying your meal
They are very specific in making your food/coffee EXACTLY how you like it- special requests are honored Mitchell made me the best dry double shot cappuccino ever!
I stayed for three days and ate exclusively there The halibut and cous cous was excellent, followed by baked chocolate cake with vanilla gelato and white chocolate crumble, amazing berry herbal tea, yum.
Day two, Risotto indescribleably good!!
Day three, Roasted chicken with pearl onions red bliss potatoes and long beans , breakfasts were organic eggs fresh baked bread fresh berries maple smoked bacon and the best hash browns I've had, yogurt with granola and berries, and cast iron baked eggs with spinach and potatoes (like a quiche) very good!

getting hungry?
Head over to OUTPOST, see you there!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Great, fresh sea food with a view to boot! Whenever I've been here it happens to be on a weekend and of course with the expected wait. Worth it every time. I could go for some of their clam chowder right now.

Categories: Bars, Mexican, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: HAIR IN 3 SEPERATE  PLATES! Food is great gone like ten times but the new young cooks hair ended up in my chips a side plate of peppers and a salad so just look at your plates good.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: Place looks much better from the outside. Service was just ok.  I got a blackened chicken salad that was just above something I'd return.  My son's meatball sandwich tasted funky to me.

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: This restaurant, hands down, has the best complimentary chips and salsa I've ever had. This alone would make me want to come back! The green salsa is surprisingly creamy yet still hot. I'm still thinking about the chips as I write this post. 

The tacos de camaron were amazing as well. The shrimp included in the tacos were almost the size of my palm!! They were very generous with the amount of shrimp per taco too. The rice was alright--definitely not the highlight of the show but did it's job.

The taquitos de pato en mole coloradito was (duck taquitos with mole) was definitely interesting. The duck was shredded and fried into taquitos, and the mole was slightly bitter. I'm glad we were able to try this but I'm not sure if we'll reorder again.

The esquites Mexicanos was delicious. The corn was perfectly seasoned with cojita cheese and sour cream. Definitely the fanciest esquites I've had. 

Overall a great experience! The food was satisfying and the service was friendly. I'd recommend coming while the sun is about to set so you can enjoy your meal with a beautiful view.

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza | Review: Nothing derails my good intentions of starting a diet like a Revolver Pizza craving! It's absolutely the BEST!! Beautifully handmade crust and fresh toppings. Not fancy just delicious. And the salads are incredible. Best Caesar with real anchovy dressing. Market salad has yummy dill ranch, shaved vegetables on yummy lettuce. I don't love the atmosphere, so we always get it to go. It's super popular so get your order in early! But it's definitely our favorite pizza in SB.

Categories: Food, Acai Bowls, Bakeries, Coffee Roasteries, Desserts, Coffee & Tea | Review: I came to Carpinteria to celebrate 4th of July, being my first time here i looked up coffee shops near me, and Lucky Llama came up, the reviews were good so i decided to try it. It did not disappoint, customer service was excellent and the place feels very cozy.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: I left this place still hungry and extremely disappointed as I had heard good things about the food.... Where is the food? Menu is very sparse and so are the servings. The waiter suggested we order the "sampler platter", which we did. We enjoyed the "meat" on the platter and asked if we could have that as a regular "entrée" item..after asking the chef? .. The waiter said we could not have any more of the meat as there was not enough?  "Not enough" summed up my experience! We ordered 2 bottles of hard cider and were brought one bottle and told they were out! Strange place .. And they could use some help with their interior design/ ambiance. Sorry folks, I just don't get the attraction.
Categories: Gluten-Free, Gastropubs, Vegan, Restaurants, Indian | Review: Just tried this spot.... so delicious. Highly suggest. Also best customer service 10/10.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Best fresh homemade salsa on Linden in Carp. Lunch and dinners always are good. Authentic, long time local Mexican food, sixteen years.

Funky old school, nice local family. Small and cozy. 
There are always lunch and dinner specials on the sign in front. Check it out. 

Local home made flavors, no worries here. 

Yumster.

Categories: Bakeries, Cafes, Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: I stopped by for cupcakes earlier today. I took 2 bites of my delicious coconut cupcake. As I was taking the third bite I saw a long blonde hair inside my cupcake. It was a disappointment because I didn't want to go back to exchange my cupcake so I just threw it out along with the other two.

Categories: Bakeries, Food Trucks, Food, Cupcakes | Review: The best cupcakes I have ever had is from Enjoy Cupcakes in the Santa Barbara Public Market downtown. They have mini cupcakes for $2 each and you can get one of each flavor (6 flavors) for $10 that comes in a cute little mini egg carton. Enjoy Cupcakes never gets boring because they change their flavors often and I have yet to try a single one that is a miss. From the Chocolate Malt sprinkled with tiny bits of In 'n Out fries to Chardonnay and the Neapolitan, the flavors can never go wrong. The problem with many cupcakes is that they're too dry and they crumble at the touch. But Enjoy Cupcakes are always perfectly baked and the gooey fillings on the inside keep it moist and scrumptious. The location is also perfect; the Public Market is a hip little indoor place with a few other good eateries like the Thai restaurant and the unique ice cream shop that I have yet to try.

Categories: Vegetarian, Gluten-Free, Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, American (New), Vegan, Cafes | Review: The food was good, at a good price.  The staff are very friendly.  However, this was not the place for us.  It wasn't clear from yelp or the website that this is just a tiny little place with only 3 tables.  We were with our 3 year old and 18 month old and there were no high chairs... so the kids had to sit on our laps and we were barely able to eat our food.   It took a really long time to get our food, and I was confused about the order in which the food was coming out... a few people who were behind us actually got their food first.  
Also, apparently the tables are also considered community tables because some lady with no personal boundaries came and sat at our four top right next to my husband and talked to us the entire meal lol.   I would've given this place four stars, but I really think it should be made much more clear in the website to not take kids here.   If you're not going to have high chairs, you have to advertise that, because as a parent it is a total nightmare to be surprised by something like that.  
I guess I was just disappointed because it was not at all what I expected based off of what I saw on yelp when I picked the place out. Also, I have no idea why yelp says this place is "good for groups."   The place could probably seat 12 people TOTAL (not a group of 12) if you're lucky.   Yelp seriously needs to revise its specifications for this restaurant.

Categories: British, International Grocery, Grocery, Food, Ethnic Food, Herbal Shops, Restaurants, Health Markets, Specialty Food, Health & Medical | Review: Bad customer service! I'm Vietnamese and the owner is Vietnamese too. The owner lady walked next to me when I was shopping and gave me a dirty look. When I checked out, she talked to me so rude, threw the receipt to my bag and didn't even said thank or goodbye. I guessed she was in bad mood? 
Never come back here again.

Categories: Cafes, Restaurants, Sandwiches, Chicken Shop, Salad, American (New), Burgers, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Got the Ahi Tuna Salad and the Earth Burger! Soo good! The salad dressing and salad was my favorite. The burger was flavorful too but the patty was a but too squishy for my liking. But the food is good price for a filling meal. Also the proceeds go back to the community which is amazing

Categories: Nightlife, Sports Bars, Bars | Review: A nice departure from the college students and pop music you find in most bars of downtown Santa Barbara. Think upscale dive bar (yes, I realize that is an oxymoron) with a friendly local crowd. Music is selected via Jukebox, a rarity in Santa Barbara.
What I like most is that instead of featuring baseball and football (American), they show rugby and soccer on their flatscreen tv's. When World Cup is on, you can count on them showing it and a packed bar.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: We had oysters on a half shell, clam chowder, salmon, sea bass, calamari... ALL tasted great. Good food, good wine, great company and very attentive waiters! Comin back!!

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: All I can say is the the view of the beach and the amazing food they make... is delicious.  Heat service as well. I was blown away. I'm usually picky about this food type but it surprised me on how flavorful it was. Not to mention... if there's a bit of a wait. You can always walk around the corner and grab a drink.
Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis | Review: Best sammies and salads!!!!  Every time I come I try a new item, never a disappointment!!!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: No dude. Just because they happen to serve random cow bits, does not automatically make this place "good" because it's "authentic"..... I had high hopes but oh well.

Categories: Steakhouses, Bars, Brewpubs, Breweries, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Sports Bars, Restaurants, Food, Burgers, Comfort Food, Seafood | Review: Totally good local spot. Great beer selection. Calamari was a little soggy but everything else was great. We had a burger and a prime rib sandwich. Both did the job. The staff was the best part. Super sweet ladies!

Categories: Custom Cakes, Bakeries, Food | Review: Amazing! We love cake and this was seriously the best cake we have ever had! The passionfruit coconut layer was to die for. All of our guests raved about it!!.

She was easy to communicate with and so sweet. The cake was delivered and decorated on site. When we saw it we were blown away. It was exactly what we wanted. 

Christine is so talented we loved that she made the cake from scratch. 

GO FOR A TASTING and you will be so happy you did!

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: I recently went here to have dinner alone at the bar and I was very happy I did! The service is phenomenal and the drinks are delicious! I ordered the chicken sandwich which I would highly recommend with spicy aoli sauce. The atmosphere is lounge vibes meets classic american style and everyone who sat around me was so friendly. They also have their own parking lot so there is no worrying about space like other parts of Montecito.

Categories: Nightlife, Restaurants, Steakhouses, Bars, American (Traditional) | Review: Eating at High Sierra (at sea level) is like eating at a Denny's with a bar.  Outside of restaurant has no landscaping and the interior is horrible. It looks like amateur decorator failed attempt.  We sat at a booth where half of the table was in glaring 80's track lighting and the other half of the table was in TOTAL darkness!  Really?!  We asked why was the lighting was so bad ? And we needed a different table.  The waitress was perplexed and had to ask a manager for help.  The food was terrible.  Ribs were obviously smoked on premises, yay! But hopelessly dry and over cooked, BBQ sauce too sweet.  Chili was terrible, as was the side salad and garlic bread.  Bacon cheeseburger was good but not special.  Tastes like they are cutting a lot of corners and not cheap as it should be.  Too bad for Goleta.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Saw this place on DDD and had to give it a try.  I ordered the regular fish and chips, a side of coleslaw, and a cup of clam chowder.  The chowder was different, but good.  It was creamy and had potato, clam, celery, and mushrooms in it.  The coleslaw is made with horseradish, and well, it's different.  Not terrible, but not great either.  The fish is overrated.  Yes, the fish itself is good, but the batter is kinda bland.  After a couple bites, the batter just peels off the fish.  The chips on the other hand were surprisingly good - usually fries are just ok, nothing special, but these were soft and well cooked.  Overall, a decent place for fresh food.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Who gives a donkey shit that some old bag who loved terrines and ugly dresses thought this was the best Mexican food around. 

And honestly the line of food tourists and middle age SB locals outside is quite obscene. Go back to your Costco cookies, frappuccinos and lobster bisque.

Anywho--the handmade tortillas are the kind of foodstuff that people dream about, and each taco creation is spectacularly fresh and unique.  This is unlike any "tacqueria" I have ever been to.  

Note--No burritos.  Know what you are going to order before getting to the window inside.  Once inside the menu is directly to the right, and then you only have a minute or so to solidify your choices before ordering.  Don't hold up the line being some hem and haw douche bag.

Categories: Specialty Food, Health Markets, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: Wow!!!  Stumbled upon this cute bakery while shopping.  Having Celiac is difficult and it's even more difficult to find something sweet and savory that fits my dietary needs.   I got loaves of bread to go and will likely regret not buying more.  While there, I got 1/2 a grilled cheese with turkey and a slice of cake.  My friend got half a Turkey sandwich with pumpkin soup.  She also got a piece of lemon cheesecake to go.  All of it was amazing!!!

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Santo Mezcal brings a new and fresh twist on Mexican cuisine from Guadalajara in the Jalisco region with amazing dishes including delicious ceviches including pulpo (grilled octopus), ahi, and halibut; empanadas, interesting and flavorful salads; highly recommended crab enchiladas; chicken moles; and a sublime osso buco; and traditional dishes including tacos and burritos. All of this fantastic food is served amidst a very chic scene replete with all the beautiful people enjoying a full bar with many original craft cocktails! A clean and contemporary build out, attentive staff and amazing location offering the opportunity to stroll on the beach after dinner makes this a must visit!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Sandwiches, American (Traditional) | Review: Came here for breakfast and it hit the spot. The country friend steak was so good and the french toast was yummy as well. The staff so nice check this place out if you are looking for so good simple breakfast.

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: This place has some really great ice cream. A great selection of cones and flavors to choose from.  My favorite is the banana salted Carmel ice cream.  Plenty of seating and things to look at.
Categories: Cafes, Restaurants | Review: Seems that there's only one regular that goes to the lounge. People come during random hours of the night

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: I was really excited about eating hear after a super Mexican craving! The restaurant was recommended to me by a Santa Barbara native with a discerning palate - especially for Mexican food - and the beautiful twinkling lights and romantic ambiance seemed to secure her review. Unfortunately, I was disappointed. The service was spotty and inattentive - even with a nearly empty restaurant. My margarita was flavorful but WAY too strong. The chips and salsa were meh - not homemade tasting, not corn, and too salty. The enchiladas were decent but not up to the quality I was expecting. It was wonderful to look around and enjoy the scenery and to sit outside on a beautiful night, but I would definitely not go back for the food.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: I was quite disappointed with the food. My husband and I came here for a romantic dinner. The location is stunning. Right next to the beach and views are amazing. I ordered a blood orange margarita and it didn't taste right. I asked them to put some additional triple spec in it and when they brought it back it tasted like water. I didn't even drink half of it. I ordered the ahi tuna with wonton chips off the happy hour menu. The tuna was good but the wonton chips were not crispy at all! Tasted stale. I asked them about it and the waiter said that they are room temperature and that's how they come! We then ordered a brownie sundae. How could you get this wrong? Brown was crunchy and not fudgey and ice cream tasted more like ice milk. My husband got the steak and he said it was ok. I know I won't go back here. Service was a bit slow too.

Categories: Steakhouses, American (Traditional), Desserts, Food, Restaurants | Review: Heard about this place for years but had never tried it until this evening. 
I can't say one negative thing about the service or food. One of the best meals
I have had. Everything from the bread to the main entree was delicious. Their baked potato was the best I have ever had. You even get homemade pie for dessert included with your meal. We will definitely be returning.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Bakeries | Review: Loved this place- during a weekend visit we were tempted to go there again- in fact, we did, and their power was out!  a coupla patrons outside had sad faces.

We didn't try their egg dishes- but the soup, salad, bread were all terrific.  Great ambience- not many tables.  I can see why this is a local favorite.  Super nice staff.

Categories: Cuban, Restaurants, Bars, Sandwiches, Nightlife | Review: I stumbled upon this place while I was having a drink at Shaker Mill. They are located in the same building but are two seperate businesses! I've only had cuban food a few times but have never had it in Santa Barbara, so I'm excited to have a new cuisine in town! They are open currently from 4pm-1am so they are open late night which is awesome! From 10pm-1am they have a more limited late night menu but they still have a cuban sandwich and the Plato Mixto on this menu with other sides as well!

I looked over the menu and had no idea what to get. Everything looked so good on the menu it was hard to decide! I ended up going with the Cubano sandwich since I was told that was the most popular. My friend got the Plato Mixto with shrimp and we got a side of the Yucca Fries to share. After we ordered we grabbed our silverware and then headed to a table. They brought the food to us which was super nice!

Our food came very quickly even though they seemed fairly busy. The Cubano sandwich was delicious!!! It was fresh and tasty! The ham and cheese were so good together I loved the sandwich and mustard aioli added a nice kick to it. The Plato Mixto with shrimp that my friend got was huge! It came with yucca fries, a salad, rice and beans. She asked for no beans because she didn't want beans but typically it comes with beans. The Yucca Fries were my favorite part!! They were cooked perfectly soft but sweet and had this creamy aioli to go with them. 

This place was so delicious I love it! I can't wait to come back here soon to try other things on their menu. Once they open for lunch I know I will be coming here all the time. I'm excited to have a Cuban restaurant now on state street! :)

Categories: Donuts, Restaurants, Bakeries, Food, Sandwiches | Review: Anna's Bakery is the best! 
When I get donuts, everyone is excited when they know the donuts come from Anna's. The  donuts are always fresh, taste delicious, and there are never any leftovers!
Also worth noting, are their oatmeal blueberry muffins, raspberry bars, and brownies.
Not only is the service always friendly, but Anna's also gives back to local youth volunteers in our community. You can satisfy your sweet tooth AND feel good about supporting local business who supports us!

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: This place is absolutely fantastic for breakfast/brunch. Service is great as well. I had the smoked bacon spinach smoked Gouda scramble. Everything very fresh. Was delicious. I highly recommend it.

Categories: Restaurants, Ethiopian, French | Review: Went for the preset Sunday menu, and just loved it. Had the perfect evening with wonderful food, wine and service. Was quite well priced for the great food.

Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Indian | Review: I ordered the classic chicken curry and garlic naan. The curry is not what you would expect of typical curry. It wasn't thick or creamy, it was thin like broth. I also asked for it to be spicy and it wasn't spicy at all. The kicker for me was that it was very expensive. I paid a little over $20 for lunch. It's okay, but I probably won't be going back.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Tex-Mex, American (Traditional), Burgers, Food, Sandwiches, Mexican, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: Yummy food, and always nice to find vegan options on the menu that are tasty! Fresh ingredients, friendly staff and great patio!

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: My friend brought me here as I was visiting her for the weekend. Upon coming, I did not have many high expectations for this place as it is located in SB. I was surprised once I tried the food and the flavors are actually comparable to some thai places located in the LA/OC area. 

We ordered the pineapple fried rice, pad thai combo, and the crying tiger. All the dishes were very good and flavorful. It came in a big portion as well. The only downside was probably with the medium rare steak being a bit too chewy. The sauce that came with it had a weird taste but somehow it worked well with the steak. 

As for the service, everything was very slow paced. We probably waited around 20 minutes to get all our orders. Overall, the food is really good but the service can be better.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Fantastic food, amazing drinks and great service -- That combo
along with reasonable prices will give you a memorable experience.

Categories: Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants, Pizza | Review: Definitely the worst pizza ordering experience I've had.

First, when I called, I was on hold for probably 5 minutes, if not longer. When they finally picked up I told them I would like a personal pan pepperoni. I wanted the personal because I was staying at a hotel and didn't feel like having leftovers. They told me that my order was less than their delivery charge, and would I like to add anything to the order? I said no, that I'll just pay the delivery charge. Then the guy, rather rudely, told me that I couldn't do that, and that I must add something else to the order. Most pizza places allow you to do this, but apparently this one doesn't. So I reluctantly changed my order to a small pizza.

When it arrived I was discovered they had gotten my order wrong. I had received a thin crust pizza instead of the pan crust which I ordered. The pizza tasted like cardboard with sauce and cheese on it. So disappointed.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Now here is a taco stand that doesn't look like much from the street, and does not have the fanciest of interiors, and has a single restroom that seems to have a small line. But who cares about that! The hand made, home made tortillas and the lovely toppings and tasty salsa bar make this a place that I will come to again and again. It is 8:30 AM and the fact is, I am now salivating and looking forward to lunch today. Guess Where?

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: Mesa Verde is hands down one of our favorite go-to spots for fresh delicious vegan fare.  Not only is their food locally sourced and delicious, but the staff is beyond welcoming and helpful.  Some of my favorites on the menu: 

The Grilled Flatbread - the caper berries are CLUTCH.  
The Cashew Grilled Cheese Sandwich is BOMB.  
Polenta Fries - YUM
Baklava - Just trust me...

I'm one of those annoying people who constantly re-order the menu items they know and love, so bear with me as we make our way through the menu!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Very good Mexican food, my wife and I were very impressed. I got the Chile Relleno burrito with bean and rice. The chili relleno tasted so fresh and had a little crunch so they didn't cook it too long. Wife ordered a chile verde burrito which was very good. Both burritos had plenty of filling and worth the money. We also ordered potato taquitos. You don't see potato taquitos very often at fast food Mexican restaurants. They were very good, plenty of potatoes, seasoned perfectly and you get three to an order.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: We happened upon this place when searching for a place to have a brief business lunch at about 11:00 a.m. on a Tuesday morning.  Some other places were still closed, but we ended up here.  We were informed that they typically didn't serve lunch until around noon, but we were all craving some lunch.  We asked nicely and they went out of their way to talk to the chefs and brought us the lunch menus so we could have our wish.  I was very impressed with the service.

The food was great as well.  We ordered various sandwiches, such as the French Dip and the burger, that were juicy and succulent.  They briefly forgot to bring the juices, or "au jus," for the French Dip sandwich, but it wasn't a big deal.  The portions were perfect, I felt comfortable and satisfied after finishing the meal.  This is a great place with a great location for anyone looking to enjoy a nice meal out on the town.

Categories: Korean, Barbeque, Restaurants | Review: Pretty good for some home cooked authentic Korean BBQ. It did feel a bit rushed with the (who I assume) owner continued to come thru and attempt to cook for us. (which is the point of being there) but we enjoyed our food and the vibe was right outta K TOWN

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Visiting in Santa Barbara for the weekend and found this spot on yelp. I always turn to yelp, especially when travelling!! My husband and I tried the Santa Barbara burrito, he thought it was solid..I thought it was okay...Not sure what made it Santa Barbara style..the avocado? The burrito was definitely not traditional or authentic mexican..not exactly what I'm used to..but it was okay...I would have tried something else but they are CASH ONLY!! And I didnt have that much cash on hand. Overall, okay.

Categories: Grocery, Specialty Food, Food, Health Markets, Local Services, Community Service/Non-Profit | Review: Live, laugh, love Co-Op. This store is a gem! The staff is great, the variety is amazing, the produce is wonderful, the prepared foods are delicious. Highly recommend.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: This place is such a time capsule. It has some odd things like they bring you out salsa bread which is sourdough bread with butter and salsa. It kind of reminds me of like a TGIF 30+ years ago and not as corporate?  Crossed with like cheers? Full bar is great, cute booths. Definitely a hidden spot for locals that have been coming for years.
Categories: Burgers, Restaurants | Review: Maybe not the healthiest but man I love a good burger& fries - they've got em!

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: This place is defintely something that I think we needed more variety of in the area. The staff was courteous, the food delicious and the atmosphere was great. 

I had the tonkotsu - black w/ pork belly. I was not disappointed. The pork was tender, the broth had lots of flavor, and the noodles were cooked perfect.

The reason for the 4 stars instead of 5, they have some kinks to work out. It was a little busy and they are really new, so it took them a long time to acknowledge we had walked in. Not a huge deal, but defintely something they will get worked out. Once seated, our food and drink orders were taken promptly, however it took them about 10+ mins to bring over 2 waters. We also had a second waiter come over, after we'd ordered, and asked to take our food and drink order. Great they they were making sure we'd been attended, but not great they weren't sure whom needed assitance (as the table next to us still needed to even have their drink order taken). 

I will defintely return soon and update my review then.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Delis, Breakfast & Brunch, Tapas/Small Plates, Cafes | Review: This is still the best restaurant in Santa Barbara!! During this past year we have brought over 80 guests here and EVERYONE has agreed with this description! Never had even a mediocre meal! We never take a chance... Always State&Fig

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Great food. Great drinks. Great place..... 
Or was.....
Need to preface that I've been here many times on many occasions including renting out their back patio for my birthday!!! I'm absolutely shocked at the service- or lack there of- from a recent visit. I found myself arguing with a hostess and a waitress about the seating availability (both of which not on the same page) to start. After finally convincing them to seat us (four people, at 7:30 on a Wednesday night) we sat for 20 minutes with ZERO contact from any of the severs. NONE. 
The decision was unanimous at the table to leave and as we did I handed the menus to the server and "thanked her". She was oblivious to the entire thing. 
Get your shit together guys. That was embarrassing

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches | Review: Love the De La Guerra. Always the perfect amount of guac and cheese. Sandwiches take a tad longer than I would hope to be brought out, put at least you can munch on some pickles or peppers while you wait. 

Haven't had a bad thing on the menu yet. My friend loved their Reuben if you're into that.

Categories: Salad, Restaurants, Pizza, Sandwiches, Italian | Review: This review is completely about the service because the food is actually 5 stars. I have been here a few times, and every time we encountered rude people. Today for example the man that took our food to our table was extremely rude, when he asked us if we needed anything else and we said we need napkins and a fork he rolled his eyes and leaves gets the stuff comes back and basically slams it on the table and asks us in such a rude way "is there anything else you need??" 
Last time we just got our food to go because again we had a rude woman helping us. 
Again the food is amazing but they need to train their employees better or get new ones that actually like their jobs.

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Sushi Bars | Review: Since COVID, we've been unable to get UNI sushi at our go too restaurants in Santa Clarita. This past weekend, we were in Santa Barbara for our anniverary and Sushi was decided as our special dinner, choosing a place that had it currently available and serving it. Local, Santa Barbara UNI is unparalleled.
Sun Sushi is off the beaten path of Santa Barbara, being 3 miles away from downtown--but this is a great thing! We were able to get a reservation on the day before at a time we wanted. Upon our arrival, the restaurant was busy but without a wait. By the time we left, every table and seat was taken at the sushi bar.
Max and his partner in business were our Sushi chefs. Both were personable as was their service staff and genuinely engaged in our experience. If there is one take away for someone reading my verbose review, let is be that THIS IS THE FRESHEST FISH WE'VE EVER HAD at a Sushi Restaurant, anywhere, including that famous chain in LA Live and in NYC.
The UNI was spectacular AND the freshest Spanish Mackerel and Red Snapper I've ever had.
We cannot say enough about an awesome experience other than that it was unfortunate that our son and sushi buddy was not there to enjoy it with us, however, a return visit is emminent so that we can enjoy it once again, with our son, too.
Tell Max Rob and Christine sent ya!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: I absolutely love Mexican Fresh. My favorite Mexican food in town. Extremely fresh, reasonably priced, and the guys who work there are great! Love this place - be sure to check it out.

Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Food is good enough, not blow your mind amazing. Decor is weird for a Thai place, really Americanized. This is a white wash Thai place and you can taste it. The waiter however, was really nice--- thank you Cody. He had to leave before closing our table and the next waiter that finished for him was terrible. 3 stars for the restaurant but +1 for Cody.

Categories: Barbeque, American (Traditional), Cuban, Restaurants | Review: So, I heard of this place through one of my good friends and he recommended this place.  I then begin ordering a pretty large order 4 adults/2 kids.  I'm on hold for 5 minutes then hang up to call back, on hold for another 5 minutes.  I finally get through.  I begin placing my order (which I repeated 3 times) and get a reasonable wait time (30-45) delivery.  I order all large sizes of damn near everything.  2 Caesar salads, southwestern corn, 2 full orders of chicken wings , mustardy potatoes, humus, and 2 orders of Mac and cheese.   I receive 1 Caesar salad with no dressing, (missing the other Caesar) no Mac and cheese, chicken wings with 1 ranch and 2 wing sauces when I asked for a whole lot more than that.  I asked for pita bread for the humus but they said it comes with a lot of the food I was ordering, it cane with 4 tiny pieces for a huge amount of humus.  I called back to fix it, and they came back with the extra Caesar, still no dressing for both now.  No pita bread, no Mac and cheese.  (They didn't put it on the initial order and I received none even after having to repeat my entire order 3 times) and 1 extra ranch for all the wings (24+)
The food was good though, not gonna lie.  I reached out to them again through the company site and no response.   I wanted to give them a chance to fix/respond to there mistakes.  This whole deal took at least 2 hours with 2 hungry toddlers that pushed there bed time back.  If I do ever decide to order from them again, it will be an order for PICK UP, NOT DELIVERY.  Just thought ya'll should know.

The owner contacted me and is gonna make things right.  Most companies don't.  Thank you Paul Freeman!  I'll try you guys out again very soon

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: I used to come here for many years and just recently returned. It is one of the very few good sit-down Mexican spots in town that doesn't require a wait. The burritos in particular are large and delicious. They also have a comprehensive menu with fajitas, shrimp dishes, and more. 

The service is excellent and it's a great place for families, as they have a nice kids menu and a friendly vibe. I also appreciate the location in the back of a quiet shopping center.

My only criticisms are that their ranch dressing is not good - tastes suspiciously like hidden valley ranch from a large Costco bottle - and that they serve their plates at roughly 2 million degrees. Seriously, warm them if you must, but nobody needs a plate that hot. I don't get it. If they changed it, I'd definitely give them my 5th star.

Categories: Caribbean, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Cajun/Creole, Vegan | Review: Good starter restaurant. Invited mycoworker and her husband for dinner not knowing or expecting anything. Turned out what we ordered was very good. We ordered fried plantations to start. I the catfish and cocnut grits, myfriends ordered Ethiopian chicken stew and. Crab and fish gumbo. All dishes were tasty well portioned. The menu is moderately prices. An application for an alcohol license was in process during our visit but a short walk to the CVS yielded 2 bottles of red.  If this is what a restaurant does off the bat then may succeed and hope they do. Go different and try Caribbean.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Great food- very nice salsa bar with unique choices (not your typical salsas at all!). Very delicious food. Had a torta & seafood burrito- both were fabulous!

Categories: American (New), Steakhouses, Tacos, Mexican, Seafood, Restaurants | Review: Terrific food, thoughtfully prepared, in an attractive and welcoming setting. Modest, but interesting and reasonably priced, wine (and beer) list. Truly excellent service. Highly recommended.

Categories: Soup, Thai, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: I am a huge lover of Thai food, or anything Asian for that matter. Naturally my freshman year I set out to find a good Thai place and wandered in here with a friend and loved it. It has been four years and many other Thai places in SB and this is still my favorite. Their Tom Ka Gai soup in unparalleled by any other and their chicken satay and yellow chicken curry are excellent as well. The service is great and I like the atmosphere.

Categories: Seafood, American (Traditional), Burgers, Bars, Nightlife, Restaurants, Cafes, Sports Bars | Review: In my opinion preferable to Brophy Bros. No complaints from me! Drinks are fantastic, food is good and service is great! Live music, the harbor, indoor/outdoor seating, just a few of the things making this the perfect destination for a chill but fun dinner out. 
ADDED BONUS I just found out this restaurant will be one of the few to pioneer the way to no straws and eliminating one use plastic waste. Take the time, look into the cause and statistics of plastic straw use. It is such a small sacrifice for such a huge impact. 
Help make our oceans a cleaner healthier place.

Categories: Desserts, Food, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, French | Review: Bottom line: they served me raw croissants twice. Once after I sent back the first one because though it appeared done on the outside, the center was RAW dough. Oh, and my crêpe was cold. And my coffee. All in all, the French Brunch special was a complete and utter disappointment. Beyond being unappetizing and inedible, the croissant snafu indicates that the chef doesn't know how to prepare the foods on his own menu. Also, they didn't offer to take them off the bill. Totally weird experience.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: I would've given it a 5 star if their servings looked as big as the pictures, especially for the popcorn crawfish. But anyway..
Ambience: 5/5
Service: 5/5
Flavor: 4/5, it could use some more soul and love
Would come again?: YESYESYES! I'm willing to give it another go, maybe their other dishes will be better!

Categories: Restaurants, Delis, Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Sandwiches | Review: The cheesecake is phenomenal. The macaron is impeccable. Waiting on the sandwiches but I'm sure there just as delicious. Just got the sandwiches. Yum! $21 for 2 sparkling waters, A chicken pesto sandwich add Brie, a cheesecake and macaroon. The service
Is amazing. Thank you!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Delis, Breakfast & Brunch, Tapas/Small Plates, Cafes | Review: We were there for their very first night of dinner service. People didn't know they were open for dinner so it was not crowded, but they had a full complement of staff.
Their staff is friendly and attentive and we swapped good hiking locations.
I had the Roasted Organic Chicken with harvest risoni, (like rissoto meets rice-a-roni in  a good way), and green olive salsa verde. The chicken was tender and delicious, but an enormous portion. Even with Chinese style sharing with my table mates I still took home a doggie box.
The menu looked delicious and I wanted to try lots of dishes, but alas my seat mates all ordered the braised short ribs, roasted carrots,soft polenta, arugula and truffled pecorino dish. The meat was packed with flavors and fall off the bone tender. I loved the polenta, carrots and all.
Nice desserts too. We were helpless to resist the flourless chocolate cake with whipped cream, not too sweet to counterpoint the sinful cake.
I liked the ambient lighting, the soft warm faux walls and high old world ceilings. I'll go again.
Categories: Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, American (New), Bars, Restaurants | Review: In a nutshell: great cocktails, adventurous  and fun menu. Our "go-to" place with out-of-town guests.

Categories: American (New), Pizza, Pubs, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Beer Bar | Review: Great vibe with excellent staff. I came here based on some of the reviews and had my mind made up on what to order before I showed up. I decided to have a light dinner of appetizers and beer; and I ended up leaving pretty full and happy without breaking the bank.

I'd definitely recommend the Brussels sprouts; they're cooked with onions and bacon for an amazing flavor. Even though the wings were tasty, I'd skip them next time because I'd question the birds they got them from due to the size and the ranch dressing seemed thinned out. Don't get me wrong; they were good, just not the best deal on the menu.

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: I came here solo for lunch around 1:00. I was seated immediately in the bar area downstairs. A man took my drink order right away. He also noticed my table was a little wobbly so he brought a wedge right over and fixed it. My server came by within minutes as well. I had the fish tacos with crispy cod. OMG it was delicious. The tortilla chips and black beans it came with were also superb. They also being 3 different bottled sauces. Since I'm a wimp I had the verde sauce on the tacos and used it for a dip. It was sooo good. Perfect place for lunch and the service was top notch!
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Always the best food! I wish I would be more adventurous but I always just get some variation of the nachos and they are spectacular!

Categories: Lounges, Nightlife, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, American (Traditional), Soup | Review: Came here for happy hour with my coworkers - one of the best happy hours in town. $5 appetizers, $4.50 margaritas... I'm down.

Happy hour runs from 4-8! Great place to come after work, but be warned that it is VERY crowded. My coworker got there an hour early to reserve seats and he still got bumped out by several groups of people. We got up for no more than 60 seconds to scope out other tables and lost our seats, so guard your table(s) with your LIFE! :-)

I tried the popcorn shrimp and shrimp quesadilla. Nothing mouthwatering, but not bad either. I liked that the quesadilla came with the guacamole and sour cream on the side since I like my quesadillas plain jane.

Service was great, too - our waitress was pretty on top of things. Asked me for my order the minute I walked through the door! Very attentive. We did have to flag her down for the check, but she was gracious enough to make separate checks for the people who had to leave early. She is also a doppleganger for an Olympic swimmer, so that was cool.

My favorite part of the experience was the giant aquarium near the front door. I love watching fishies!

Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Himalayan/Nepalese | Review: Walked by this place and decided to give it a shot!  It didn't disappoint any one of us.  My son cannot handle spicy food, so this place was perfect for him as you can order your food with the level of spiciness from 1 to 10.  We ordered mango lassi, lamb and chicken momos, and non vegetarian Thali, which came with a meat curry of your choice(chicken, lamb, or goat), naan, rice, daal, and paneer, and rice pudding at dessert.  Everything was simply delicious.  The naan was particularly memorable, as I've never had such yummy naan before.  It came straight from the oven, crispy on the outside, and soft on the inside.  The price may seem like they r on the higher side, but the portions are just huge!  

They do have indoor and outdoor dining.  All tables were spaced out 6 ft apart.  The server was very helpful.  If we ever visit again, we are coming back to this place!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: This place can be a hit and a miss.
The happy hour is really good I can't complain. My favorite drink is the Paloma. It's a Margarita with grape fruit! OMG to die for!! Happy Hour all day Sunday is great!!!
The service and the food is where i only give this a 3 star. Service is only ok when sitting at the bar. There is a awesome bartender named Dominic so hope you get him!  The food is ehhhh.....not that great but not that bad. In the middle. I would go there for the happy hour and drinks :)

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Food Poisoning. Never again will I return. Tried to like this place after a few visits, but then a week ago I got horrible food poisoning (it was the only thing I had eaten that day) Also, the staff is very standoffish and comes across as rude. I waited over half an hour for a stinkin' BRC burrito one time! Really?!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Get the salsa aguacate! Medium heat with avocado blended in, my favorite! Taco Tuesday is a good deal but I usually go for sharing a Chile relleno burrito with someone whenever possible. They have a delicious salad dressing and side salad option if you're not a big rice and beans fan. Delicious thin and crispy chips and salsa bar.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Nice little deli near shopping district.  Seating inside or outside but limited.  Had a Pickles and Swiss Sandwich.  Wife had Veggie Sandwich.  Great!

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: Our favorite Santa Barbara tacos. We love the triple play-three tacos of your choice for $9. We love the fish taco, bahn mi and crispy taco combination. 

The ahi taco is out of this world too. Tough taco decisions, but so good!

The people taking the orders are quite  unfriendly, but thankfully you don't have to deal with them much.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Restaurants, Cafes, Food, Acai Bowls, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: This place is amazing!! Might be considered a little pricey, but you pay for quality. Plus if you love açaí toppings, totally worth it. You get to pick a size açaí bowl or smoothie and then you get unlimited toppings. The best part is they layer the toppings in a tall container instead of a wide bowl, so you keep getting new delicious toppings as you make your way through the bowl. I love açaí bowls, but I'm usually stuck with half of the açaí left and no toppings at other restaurants. At this one, I had peanut butter and Cocoa nibs in every bite. The açaí was the perfect amount of sweet and wasn't icy. The employees were all very nice and helpful. They explained their menu and answered our questions since it was our first time. There are plenty of tables to sit at outside and we got free parking at a public lot a block away. I will definitely come back here next time I'm in Santa Barbra

Categories: Sandwiches, Wraps, Kebab, German, Restaurants | Review: First time I came here, my husband and I had a wonderful experience. The two gentleman in the back were on top of proving customer service and explained to us how we order. I like how we can choose our meat, sauces and veggies and things we didn't want. When we got our food, it was delicious and were so full that we had a hard time eating our fries. 
I had recently ordered the second time, but through SB menus and the taste and quality were still the same.

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: This review is only for their princess cake. Such a disappointment for a $12 slice of cake. I love princess cake and always look for a good one. I was so excited to see this bakery but what a disappointment. Don't waste your $12. Sad.

Categories: Tapas/Small Plates, Desserts, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Seafood | Review: Well we were disappointed. My fish was ok. Not the grand experience we had our first time. My wife's seafood pasta was ok. The appetizers were not anything to write about. I would say it was rather 'blah' of an experience.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Food is a four but parking is a one
Sam's Goleta totals out to a three. Unless you can walk then it's a five since you'll burn off the yummy sandwich on the way back to the office.

Very friendly family serving up great sandwiches made to order. I lean toward a medium #11with cotto salami, mortadella, genoa, capicola and provolone. 

Service with a big smile while the TV is usually on soccer 
On a business lunch, enjoy a very limited selection of beer in the bottle.
Parking in the back is limited and full at lunch. I usually double park and run in. 

My advice is to get Sam's To Go and roll to the beach. Relax with your good food and enjoy life. You deserve it.

Categories: Wine Bars, Bars, Beer Bar, Nightlife | Review: I was so sad that they were not serving frozé! :( I saw all the Yelp pictures and drove out a little further just to have one! I'm sad there was no therapy for me! :/ 
Lol so even though we weren't hungry, we figured we eat something and drink whatever since we were already here. 
I ordered the peanut butter bacon jelly something burger, something like that ask about it. I really enjoyed the kale salad that came along with it. I had some vino and ordered a few rounds. Service was great, place was clean and the vibe is good. 
Sadly, it wasn't frozé all day for me.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: The Best Tacos and Salsa in Santa Barbara and Goleta!
Fresh, Fast, and very Friendly.
Enuf said!
Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: this place is dank. don't believe the haters.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Staff from Friday July 16th was incredible! So friendly, so attentive, super friendly and efficient! We love going there for the food and the drinks and the staff really just makes the experience so much better!

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: Only been there twice...tonight and nearly a year ago when they first opened...food is very good.... service awesome....Eric was our server a yr ago and tonight......he remembered me :)......best thing about tonight was Eric.....lovely smiling face friendly manner very professional.    We will be back soon.....only if Eric is our server......Connie from Sicily :)

Categories: Grocery, Food | Review: The meat section of this grocery store has the most amazing marinated meats; they are super fresh and they are super delicious.  If you want to do your own tacos at home or for friends; this is the place I always go.  The market is pretty small and doesn't have a huge selection itself; but I don't expect it to.  But the attention they give their meat department is a great bargain for what you pay.

Categories: Tuscan, Seafood, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife, Italian | Review: Went for lunch yesterday and it was excellent.  I've been to Italy twice.  I consider myself a food snob.  And this was as good as Italian food gets.

We ordered the Paparadelle Bolognese and the Pollo Milanese.

Both dishes were terrific.  Great flavor, great presentation, and the right sized portions.

Every time I am in Santa Barbara, we will eat there.  The staff was great and the service was perfect.

I've seen negative reviews and I cannot speak to those.  However, when you make a reservation at 8 PM on a Saturday, it is an ESTIMATE that your table will be ready then.  It is not a guarantee.  Keep that in mind.

When you are accustomed to chain restaurants who fall all over themselves to make even the biggest jerk happy, don't expect the same thing from a place that is independent and doesn't really need your attitude or business.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Burgers, American (New) | Review: We had an awesome experience here. Sat outside with the doggie basking in the warm sun. Try their specialty bourbon drinks. They are winners!

Categories: Food, Vegetarian, Salad, Restaurants, Cafes, American (Traditional), Burgers, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: Natural Cafe is a great place for vegetarians or those who are looking for a meal on the healthier side. 

The new location in the Camino Real marketplace is right next to Hollister Brewing. You order at the counter and take a seat inside (it's not an overly large seating area) or outside on the patio, where they have tables with umbrellas and heat lamps for the evenings. 

The menu of appetizers, salads, burgers, pizzas, pastas, etc. has something for pretty much everyone. I like the Yogi Special (sautéed tofu, brown rice, ample side salad with your choice of dressing that comes on the side) and the Zen Burger (veggie patty burger which comes with chips and salsa) also looked yummy. Friends ordered salad entrees and they were enormous! They don't skimp on the portions here. 

I will definitely be back when I'm in the mood for a casual, healthy lunch or dinner in Goleta.

Categories: Food, Coffee & Tea | Review: The title don't lie. This place has good coffee. You wouldn't go into a place that's called "Mediocre Cup" or "Crappy Cup," wouldja? That's how we felt, and therefore felt safe going into Good Cup as opposed to the Starbucks across the street.

Latte = solid. Not too milky, strong, and served extra hot. Red Eye = coffee + espresso shot, Pamela D. enjoyed. Lotsa couches, friendly staff, newspapers strewn about, random trinkets for sale like cutesy day calendars and "Satan sticky notes" (yea, I'm not sure what those were about either). 

Also, we agreed that it's important to travel with someone who matches your same appreciation (i.e. addiction) for coffee. Non-addicted folks just don't understand.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: This restaurant came highly recommended by a friend who travel a lot.  Once again, he nailed it.

Just off the beaten path of State street, this quaint restaurant proved to be worth every penny.  With an award winning wine list, this Italian style eatery was exceptional in every way.  All waitstaff had genuine Italian accents and paid attention to every detail.  The good was fresh, flavorful and prepared to perfection.  The beef tenderloin on a potato tart was excellent with good sized portions.   

At the waiters suggestion.  We had chocolate banana pudding for dessert, which was more like a cake.  It melted in our mouths and is a Chocolate lovers dream.  

Start to finish a fantastic experience for two with one glass of wine. $130 but worth it

Categories: Steakhouses, Cafes, American (Traditional), Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Sandwiches | Review: Left a ton to be desired, showed up at the bar, Offered a drink after 5 mins of being here. Finished the drink and once the bartender showed up to ask me how it was, I was finally able to inquire about food. This was about 20-25 mins into sitting at the bar. Finally got the food, never asked how it was until I finished it. Then sat around for another 15 mins after my food and last drink where clearly done to even get a check. Save the time and energy and go to Jack in the Box, better service and you won't pay a ton. Surprised this place has lasted since 1928. I wish I could tell you the restaurant was full and he was slammed but maybe half full and the time I was waiting for the check, I was the only one at the bar.
Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), French, Restaurants | Review: Lovely lunch, as always. Wonderful service -- Teryhus was knowledgeable and attentive.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: I was taken out to this restaurant for a date, so I'm already biased in this review. 
The atmosphere was whimsical with the outside patio lighting and candles on the tables. My date and I had the option of a booth or table and we chose a table. I looked at all of the booths, and there were 3 booths each occupied with a couple sitting side by side. I wish I took a pic because it was so romantic. 
For the the food, I ordered the Eggplant Genovese which was an excellent choice. I can't go into great detail about it, by I wasn't disappointed.
Given that I don't know the Santa Barbara area very well, I'd definitely come back to this Italian restaurant.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: I've been a long time fan and patron of the sister restaurants, but have since moved away. I had a 3:15 train to catch, and made it here at 2:45. I let the hostess, Lidia, know my situation and she personally walked to the kitchen and let them know I had a tight schedule. They made my food in 4 minutes with no dip in quality and I was on my way. 

Commitment to quality and service like this is why this family of restaurants is worth your business and why I always stop here when I'm visiting my old home. I'll be a customer for life! Great to see that nothing has been lost in the new, upscale offshoot.

Categories: Diners, Salad, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: Anyone traveling to Santa Barbara must try Sambo's! Not only is the spot iconic. It was excellent food and even better service. There may be a line out the door but it's totally worth the wait!

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services | Review: I've wanted to give a good review for one of our neighborhood restaurants, but after time and time again trying meals at via Maestra, whether it be breakfast, lunch or dinner, it's been a consistent disappointment. Vagueness in the menu description, poor quality food & execution of dishes, as well as spotty service make it impossible for me to choose it as a restaurant I want to support.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: Lemon grass salmon. Delicious. Very comfortable with great service. We stop here the 3-4 times we come to Santa Barbara. We look forward to it each time.

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: From the martinis in mason jars to the muffin sampler to dessert, everything is good here. You'll even get entertainment while you wait from the live band outside. Colorful decor, friendly service, raucous atmosphere with sing-alongs. Be hungry when you go here because you will want to try everything. My only regret here is when I can't finish dessert....

Categories: Italian, American (New), Restaurants | Review: What other craft restaurants want to be when they grow up. All aces.

With reservations made (highly suggested) we arrived to a packed house of a mix of your usual Santa Barara suspects. ALl walks of life with many in their 40s-60s but hip. These are not borring grandparents and fit right in with 30 Somethings. While you don't need to dress to impress, this is not a shorts, tank top and flops. Put some effort into your denim ensamble. 

We arrived a bit early to check out the bar and decided to skip the table and eat right there. Kyle and Jodie took fantastic care of us recommending wine tastings and more. After discussing the distinctive, micro distilled alchy bottles, our bar keep took us on a tasting of Hophead vodka and Don Julio 70. Both outstanding. This is service on the rocks, over the top.

While I had my eyes set on the lasagna, sadly, they were out. With all fresh and locally obtained ingredients, sometimes it just donen't work out. Next on the list was the Tagliatelle, reved by Jodie across the marble but when I found the Whole Fish was  Branzino, there was no looking forward. That paired with Brussels sprouts and I had my Main picked out. To start, I'm all in for the Porchetta di Testa. The results were very good. The Porchetta di Testa was a mouth watering crab cake side puck o love. Bravo Jason. The fish was very good. Don't get me wrong, it was very good but did not stand up to Justin over at Julienne. The sprouts were served in a little, cast iron skillet and melted in my mouth. Certainly a re-eat.

What did she order? A Chopped salad and the Shrimp pizza. Both were delicious but I thing my choices were more exciting.

To rap this up, Anchor is a top restaurant sitting pretty with just a few Santa Barbara elite. You will want to keep coming back...just like me. The cost of admission is what you make it. There are less expensive options on the menu but you'll want to eat high on the hog. I'm told the Pork Belly Sandwich is must at lunch. I'm sold.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Bakeries, Desserts, Food Delivery Services, Patisserie/Cake Shop | Review: So far we've only had really great experiences with Insomnia Cookies. The cookies are absolutely amazing, and if you sign up to their program you get a lot of good deals. I ordered on the app and they were delivered very quickly. Looking forward to satisfying our future cookie urges with this place!

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: What a wonderful restaurant, we loved the decor and the use of repurposed materials. The staff was incredible, we had Norman waiting on us and he is great, made sure to check on our food allergies and a wealth of information on the dishes and drinks. The menu is full of great options and I'm sure we'll come back to try the dishes we didn't get to try. Thank you for such a beautiful dining experience and helping us celebrate our anniversary

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Came here because the wait was out the door for Tupelo Junction Cafe. Andersen's is a delightful spot to people watch. Brunch options were limited, but quite tasty. They have a large staff that is always ready to help - every server is available to any table at any time. They're clearly a well oiled machine with a happy staff.

I look forward to coming back here to give their dessert selection a try.

Categories: Restaurants, Cafes, Breakfast & Brunch, Cajun/Creole | Review: Hubby and I had breakfast here while we were visiting the area.  Based on the reviews, I was expecting something better.  Maybe it was an off day or something.  I had the Cajun-Mexican Special Omelet (chorizo, mushrooms, jack cheese) with hash browns and tortillas on the side.  The egg part of the omelet was overcooked and kind of rubbery and was filled with WAY too much mushroom.  It could have used a bit more of the chorizo.  I wonder what makes this dish 'Cajun-Mexican' anyway...  Hubby had the Cajun Pizza which sorta looked like it had a potato base covered with misc veg and topped with eggs and cheese.  I had contemplated ordering that when I looked at the menu.  Hubby said it was just 'alright'.

It was an OK breakfast, but nothing I'd care to repeat.  Too bad, 'cause I really dug the assortment of hot sauces and the bottle of garlic sauce they have on each table.  (Didn't try the garlic sauce.  Maybe I should have.)

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Nightlife, Burgers, Pizza, Asian Fusion, Ramen, Cocktail Bars, Dim Sum, Bars, Pubs, American (Traditional), Japanese | Review: At Last, Santa Barbara has a wonderfully new restaurant that truly offers extremely delicious Ramen.  This is coming from someone that lived in New York City for six years and grew up in The Washington D.C. Area and has been to the top Ramen Shops in both cities often.  I moved to Santa Barbara in 2003 and quickly realized that Ramen would not be a weekly dining option as it was when I lived in the East Coast.  My love for a delicious bowl of Ramen was put on hiatus as I explored my new city and enjoyed what this city had to offer.  I adapted quickly and found my new dining staples, The Shellfish Company, The Santa Barbara Chicken Ranch, Arrigato, The Boathouse, and City Kitchen(I miss you so much!).  Fast forward to March 2016, and I now have Yankee Noodle in my top 6 must support restaurants.  Let's start with Yankee Noodle's service.  When you come to Santa Barbara from restaurant rich cities like Manhattan and D.C. you have to realize that you are simply not going to get consistently good service that's offered in these cities, simply because Santa Barbara can't sustain service industry professionals.  Therefore, the restaurants mostly hire college students.  That being said and having adjusted my grading of service accordingly, I was impressed with the staff of the newly opened restaurant.  They all were very friendly, made me feel welcome, and offered great suggestions on drinks and dining options. Anyone who knows me will tell you that I ask a lot of questions. I had a really nice conversation with the Bartendar, Mirch, about the high quality Beer, Sake, and Cocktail offerings on the menu. Mitch the Bartender came to Yankee Noodle from The Goodland in Goleta.  I started with the "Lemon in the Grass" cocktail.  It's Lemongrass infused Vodka with Ginger and Kumquat syrup. It was the perfect start to my dining experience and gave me something to savor while I examined the very unique menu.  For my first dish, I choose the "Umami Fresh Corn off the Cobb".   I enjoyed every spoonful of the r.  Next I had the "Rich Savory Smokey Pork Broth".   The Broth is hearty, rich, smokey and buttery delicious.  The noodles were authentic and perfectly prepared in soft and chewy Al-dente style with big egg flavor.  You can definitely taste the complexity of flavors from the broth that takes 24 hours to make.  The toppings it came with were excellent.  The Chasu (Pork Belly) was prepared nicely. It was an ample portion that I greatly enjoyed and the cost $12.95 was typical of what you'd find in a top Ramen shop in NYC or D.C.  I took home leftover Ramen and it was just as enjoyable heated up on the stove the next day.  Yankee Noodle occupies the former Space of Union Ale.  They did a nice job on the conversion.  They fashioned a community table from a "Chop Suey" sign that was from a Santa Barbara Restaurant on State Street in 1949's.  It's a great lunch spot while enjoying the Funk Zone or dinner spot Apres Wine with friends or solo.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, American (New), Wine Bars, Nightlife, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Event Planning & Services | Review: Jane is so good! It's my boyfriend's favorite restaurant so we go for pretty much every special occasion. Every server we've had has always been friendly and professional. The food always comes to the table in a timely manner and is hot. The upstairs seating area is so cute and the chicken piccata is the best I've ever had. 

Definitely better to have reservations than to be a walk in as they've been busy every time we've gone.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Restaurants, Nightlife, Caterers, Italian, Bars | Review: My boyfriend and I were looking for a restaurant for dinner that had great food and a good vibe. A local recommended we try Chase. Unfortunately, Chase was the complete opposite of what we were looking for. Our server was neither friendly or attentive, the manager was busy chatting it up with some friends and playing on his phone the entire two hours we were at the restaurant. I ordered the shrimp piccata, and it was one of the worst meals I've ever had. The pasta tasted like plastic and the sauce had to have been from a can. I would not recommend this place for more than a quick drink at the bar.

Categories: Organic Stores, Caterers, Grocery, Specialty Food, Event Planning & Services, Health Markets, Food | Review: I'm going to start with a 3 rating (average) and add/subtract points based on my experience and we will see where it winds up! 

I found myself shopping here with no mask or gloves during the pandemic and was pleased to find disinfectant wipes by the carts outside. (+1)

I hoped to find some bread flour, but held out little hope, since there was none in town. I couldn't believe my lucky stars to find 4 bags of Bob's organic bread flour plus several other smaller King Arthur bags, which I picked up for a baker friend. That Bob's has kept me baking for 3 weeks now, and I still won't need more flour for another few weeks. (+2)

I followed suggestions from all over, where they recommend you shop for your elderly neighbors. So I had asked all the neighbors around us if I could get some groceries for them. I had lists from 4 families plus us and went about picking up peanut butter, pasta, cheeses, butter, etc. I noticed a sign about being able to buy limited quantities and realized we wouldn't be able to buy everything for our elderly neighbors, if we had to adhere to the limits. So I spoke to the manager and offered to show him the emails from our neighbors with their lists. He hoped to accommodate us and called someone else, above him, I figure. Then, the manager came back and said he was instructed not to waive the limits, even though people are encouraged to shop for their elderly neighbors. The upshot of that was that we were able to get buy most of our elderly neighbors' shopping lists, but we were not able to buy enough for ourselves due to the limits. It was disappointing that the person the manager called wasn't empowered to be flexible during a global pandemic to accommodate someone who was shopping for multiple elderly neighbors. (-2)

Whole Foods has the best big beans on their olive bar. Much better than Lazy Acres' big beans. I was thrilled to find some. But the damn bagger obviously didn't care about his work. I got home and found the plastic tub of beans had been put on its size on the bottom of the paper bag, next to fresh tomatoesalso packed under boxes of pasta and cans of beer. The tomatoes were bruised and broken, and worse, the marinated beans had leaked their oily vinaigrette all over the bottom of the grocery bag, onto the back of my car, and all over several pasta boxes and Italian paper packages of pasta that my neighbors had ordered. It was embarrassing taking greasy soiled packages of pasta to my neighbors, because the bagger didn't care. (-1)

Looks like we found our way back to a 3.

7/22/20 I'm adding another point, because of my recent shopping experience at Whole Foods. I was trying to get in and out quickly and just as I was getting in the checkout line, I remembered I had forgotten to get fancy crackers. I steered my cart towards the wine and cheese section and zipped around the corner into wine and BLAMO! I slammed into a display of cases of rosé that were stacked just shy of the end aisle. I knocked over an open case and the 5-6 bottles that were on top of the stack.

It's hard to say how many bottles of rosé I broke all over the floor of the wine section. More than 5, less than 10. 

It was horrifying as staff came running and all the customers in the long checkout line glared at me through their masked faces. I apologized up one side and down the others, offering to pay for my damage. I offered the guy cleaning it up. I offered the woman who closed off the area. I offered the manager. I offered the cashier. Everyone was unbelievably kind and insisted I not worry about it. "It happens all the time," they said, but I can't imagine they are ankle-deep in rosé that often. So, I'm adding a point back. 

We're now at 4.

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Sandwiches, Steakhouses, Restaurants, Bars, Nightlife | Review: My wife and I both work in the restaurant industry and have become accustomed to a certain level of quality when choosing to dine out. We could not speak higher of the service and food perfection we received from Opal. It is so difficult to celebrate special occasions during quarantine, and they were willing to go above and beyond to help us make our day exceptional.
